name: create
context: Create Assistants API threads with optional initial messages, metadata, and tool resources
info: Create a new thread via `POST /v1/threads`. Threads are lightweight conversation containers — create one per conversation or user session. Optionally pass initial messages to pre-load context. Threads support metadata (up to 16 key-value pairs) for tracking session state, user IDs, or topic labels. Tool resources (vector stores for file search, code interpreter files) can be attached at thread creation. The returned thread object contains an `id` used for all subsequent message and run operations. Threads persist until explicitly deleted with `DELETE /v1/threads/{thread_id}`.

sections.title: Basic creation
sections.body: Call `client.beta.threads.create()` with no arguments for an empty thread. The response contains `id` (thread identifier), `created_at` (unix timestamp), `metadata` (empty object), and `tool_resources`. The thread ID is required for all subsequent operations: adding messages, creating runs, and reading responses.

sections.title: Initial messages
sections.body: Pass a `messages` array to pre-load the thread. Each message needs `role` ("user") and `content` (string or array of content parts). Messages can include `attachments` with `file_id` and `tools` array. This is useful for providing context before the first run, such as user requirements or uploaded file references.

sections.title: Thread metadata
sections.body: The `metadata` field accepts up to 16 key-value pairs (keys max 64 chars, values max 512 chars). Use metadata to track user IDs, session identifiers, topic labels, or any application-specific state. Metadata is returned with thread retrieval and can be updated via `client.beta.threads.update()`.

sections.title: Tool resources
sections.body: Attach tool resources at thread creation for file search and code interpreter. For file search: `tool_resources.file_search.vector_store_ids` (array of vector store IDs). For code interpreter: `tool_resources.code_interpreter.file_ids` (array of file IDs). These resources are available to all runs on the thread.

links.context: Assistants API overview and assistant creation

related.note: After creating a thread and adding messages, create a run to execute the assistant
